text: Static cling, I think. There's something faintly absurd about it, the way a freshly dried shirt clutches at itself, crackling with small protests when you pull it apart. I like the texture of that resistance, the way fabric briefly forgets it is supposed to be soft and behaves instead like something alive and contrary. Running a hand over it raises the fine hairs on your arm, an intimate little static charge. It delights me precisely because it is so trivial, a tiny argument the material has with itself, settled in a moment and forgotten almost before it begins.
